articles.title: Karnataka HIV worry: 56,406 people may still be unaware of status - News9live
articles.full_text:
Karnataka HIV worry: 56,406 people may still be unaware of status
Karnataka’s MAS campaign is focusing on early HIV testing, counselling and treatment support. KSAPS says 2,05,350 people are on ART in the state, while 56,406 may still not know their HIV status.
Bengaluru: Karnataka has stepped up its HIV awareness and testing drive under the Mobilisation for AIDS Suraksha campaign, with officials focusing on people who may still not know their HIV status. The campaign is being run by the Karnataka State AIDS Control Society, also known as KSAPS.
As per official data shared till June 28, 2026, around 2,05,350 HIV-positive people in Karnataka are currently on antiretroviral therapy. The bigger concern for health officials is the estimated 56,406 people who may be living with HIV without knowing it. That is the group the campaign wants to reach first.
Karnataka pushes “Know Your Status” message
The MAS campaign is built around the message “Know Your Status”. The idea is simple. Get tested, know early, start treatment if needed, and avoid fear.
Health officials say HIV can now be managed better if detected early. Treatment through ART helps people live longer and healthier lives. It also reduces the risk of passing the virus to others when the person takes medicines regularly and keeps the viral load under control.
The campaign is also trying to reduce stigma. In many homes and offices, HIV is still spoken about in whispers. That fear often stops people from getting tested. KSAPS wants testing to be seen as a normal health check, not a reason for shame.
Breakfree QR code
A major feature of the campaign is the “Breakfree QR Code”. People can scan it to get HIV risk information and connect with confidential counselling and testing centres.
The campaign has urged adults to know their HIV status. It has also asked pregnant women to undergo HIV and syphilis testing. People with symptoms of sexually transmitted infections have been advised to seek early treatment.
What the numbers show
In 2025-26, more than 37.57 lakh people from the general population were tested for HIV in Karnataka. Of them, 11,322 new positive cases were detected.
Nearly 11.98 lakh antenatal mothers were tested during the same period, with 451 new positive cases found. Another 1,009 known HIV-positive pregnant women were followed through care.
In the first two months of 2026-27, 4.11 lakh people from the general population were tested, and 2,057 positive cases were detected.
